paper_title: Transient Simulations and Model Analysis of Past Climate Dynamics

methods.description: A method to perform transient simulations of the past 3 million years using the CLIMBER-2 model by splitting the simulations into shorter intervals to avoid long-term drifts and manage computational resources.

steps.description: Run many model simulations for 500 ka starting at different times from 3.25 Ma ago until 150 ka ago at time intervals of 100 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: execute multiple 500 ka model simulations at staggered intervals

steps.description: Initialize all model runs using identical preindustrial interglacial conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize model runs with preindustrial conditions

steps.description: Discard the initial 100 ka of each simulation as model spin-up and analyze the remaining 400 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze model output after discarding initial spin-up period

steps.description: Compute the mean over the ensemble members at each point in time to derive a continuous time series.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean of ensemble simulations for continuous time series

methods.method_name: Transient Model Simulations for Quaternary Analysis
methods.description: A technique to perform multiple simulations covering the entire Quaternary period using the CLIMBER-2 model, focusing on the robustness of glacial cycles and reducing model drifts.

steps.description: Perform more than 1000 model simulations, each 500 ka long, starting from the same initial conditions but at different points in time.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct over 1000 simulations of 500 ka each from varied start points

steps.description: Analyze the robustness of glacial cycles by testing whether different simulations converge to the same solution.
steps.searchable_summary: validate convergence of glacial cycle simulations

methods.description: Configuration and application of the CLIMBER-2 model, including the SICOPOLIS ice sheet model, to simulate past climate dynamics with a focus on the carbon cycle and radiative forcing.

steps.description: Use the Earth system model CLIMBER-2, incorporating the SICOPOLIS ice sheet model, to simulate the last eight glacial cycles.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate glacial cycles using CLIMBER-2 and SICOPOLIS

steps.description: Compute the effective CO2 concentration used in the radiative scheme to account for methane and nitrous oxide effects.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate effective CO2 concentration for radiative forcing

steps.description: Estimate benthic δ18O from modeled sea level and deep ocean temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate benthic δ18O from sea level and temperature
